title: Robustly Garlicky French Rye Souffle
description: A traditional Normandy dish with a twist, this souffle combines the earthy flavor of rye with the pungency of garlic, perfect for a comforting meal on a sick day. The cedar plank adds a unique smoky flavor to the dish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, combine the melted butter, minced garlic, and heavy cream, and cook over low heat until the garlic is fragrant.
In a large bowl, whisk together the rye flour, shredded gruyere cheese, salt, and black pepper.
In a separate bowl, whisk the egg yolks until they are pale and thick, then add the cooked garlic mixture and whisk until combined.
Add the egg yolk mixture to the rye flour mixture and stir until just combined.
In a separate bowl, beat the egg whites until they are stiff and hold their shape, then gently fold them into the rye flour mixture.
Spoon the mixture onto a cedar plank lined with parchment paper, shaping it into a round souffle.
Bake the souffle for 35 minutes, or until it is puffed and golden brown.
Remove the souffle from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
